name: Tsuro - The Game of The Path

reviews.review_description: Used to work great for me, but lately it does not track the controllers correctly and puts me in the floor even with height boost. No other app I have does this. Sad as I love the game.

This is a perfect example of how a great board game can be turned into a great VR game. It's extremely customizable so all of the little things that I found to be slightly annoying I was able to get rid of. It's very relaxing, very fun, very immersive, and very challenging. There's an incredible amount of value for the price. I can't recommend it highly enough.

I really like this game, it's very relaxing and the graphics are charming albeit a bit old school. I would easily rate this 4 or even 5 stars except for one simple fact. There is some sort of bug which will not allow me to play against anything except the "silly" ai. I have won scores of games, and still no "clever" ai unlock. It is a great game but playing against the "silly" ai over and over is just too easy

It is a nice change from Walkabout Mini Golf to play a brainteaser game with my brother. 
The multi player setup is not intuitive unfortunately.  It gets tricky setting up initially and 
after playing a match we both get a prompt to play a rematch but if we both click it we get 2 new games which is clunky to manage.  
If only one of us clicks rematch then the other one has no prompt to accept or to exit the game stage to begin a new one other than to start a new game. So that is a bug that could be addressed for improved mechanics.  
The graphics are pretty and the environment is relaxing. 
We also wish for the option to add AI players to the multiplayer match like we can in Settler's of Catan VR.  That would add a nice challenge to multiplayer.

Great game but it's hard to find each other to play again, so we give up and quit. Can you find a better way to get connected with are friends? It's just too, hard to play another game with my friends. The game once you find everyone to play, it's a great game.
Love the VR version of Tsuro, I play with my good friend quite often! We would love to see some of the other Tsuro game versions released for VR. we would love to play those  tooo!

This is a relaxing game in an Asian environment. I like that you can walk around the environment and even watch the playground from the roof. The details, the floating tree leaves and animals also lift the mood. If you really like board games, you will enjoy it, otherwise, you can get bored quickly. This game is also good for just meditating in a pleasant environment. Actually, I bought the game because of the beautiful Asian environment and to have a board game in VR. The graphics could easily be better, but it's still nice. It's worth it on sale.

I bought the game when it came out just to have a board game but totally pulled me and sticked.
Still my favourite ✌️

I don't know why people complain about multiplayer. It's really easy to play with a friend. Just start a party in Quest home and then start the game. Click the flag with the globe icon left of the settings box and start a multiplayer game and invite your friend.  
It's a great game which is easy to learn but hard to master.

You don't see the other players, it's hard to tell who's turn it is and it is ALWAYS painful to actually get into a game with friends. When it works it is fun but wow, I'd much rather use the non-vr version to play.

reviews.review_title: I'd give the game more strars if..
reviews.review_description:
1. Movement around the game space is difficult to master. Using the thumb stick to choose which direction you want your perspective focused, once you hit the trigger, is difficult to master, especially if you back yourself into a corner. I am already used to just using the thumb stick to teleport around in other games and then using the thumb stick to turn around in place. Why couldn't the developers had kept it the same as other games I've already played?

2. Multi-player is also difficult to get used to. My wife talked me into getting this game so we could play together. At one point we were able to get into it together, but when we wanted a rematch it didn't work and we ended up in separate single player games. Then we had to quit our separate games so she could invite me again. At one point I was unable to highlight the "world banner" to get into online games and join, so I didn't know what I was doing wrong. I decided to quit the game and restart, then it worked. I think it would be easier to get a normal notice in the oculus menu and chose it to join the game.

3. The perspective you get of the board, especially in the seated position, is kinda hard to see all the lines the rock would slide on once you set your tile. Is there any way you could get a birds-eye perspective of the board, so you can see the lines easier?

Other than that it is a fun, relaxing game. I like the surroundings and the zen like music.

New edit: 
So I found out that you can port to the tops of some of the buildings. I've been playing now, from the top of the roof on the left hand side of the main pagoda. It was difficult, at first, to figure out how to position myself on the roof, but now I have a much better view of the board. 
Now that my wife and I have been playing for a while, we have figured out how to successfully get into the same game together. The rematch option still doesn't work for us, but at least we can both end the game after a win and start another together. 
So at this point I'm going to give this game 4 stars.
This game is a must it’s fun relaxing and you have to find stone that are hiding. I love this game

Beautiful game and marvelous experience but terrible UI and buggy multiplayer make this game only one star.

Having to spend 30 minutes to get a game to work with family is a deal breaker.

It's easy to get started but takes a lot of time to master it. Great that the music and environment relax you as your mind tries to work out the best moves. A few issues though:
1. The graphic on the game board is confusing, particularly if you're using the red or white pieces. It serves no purpose so get rid of it when a game starts!
2. I wish the fox would appear more often. I got too close to it and now it rarely appears, and just on the porch of the pagoda. More animals in general would be nice around the periphery.
3. I wish ALL games would would provide better instructions and strategy guides. It may all seem obvious to the developers but it's not to a new user.
4. I agree with others that the resolution could be better and navigating should include more buttons so you can't accidentally hold a button too long and get an undesired effect.

Overall though, I love this game.

There are a few changes that would make this game the Zen-like experience that it deserves to be, and I am surprised that it hasn't been changed yet.
1. The button that you use to teleport should NOT be the same button that you use to choose and place tiles.
Please change the teleport button to be the 'A' button. Simple fix for one of the most frustrating elements.
2. Creating multiplayer games is needlessly confusing. If I choose a rematch, it should offer the rematch to the other person, then put us both into the same game, but it doesn't.
Sometimes multiple games end up being created and there is no clear indication of which game your friend is actually in, or clear way to remove unwanted online matches.
3. The resolution could be higher on the Quest 2. It seems like quite a low resolution game and would benefit from the higher resolution.
4. It would be really nice to actually have a physical avatar in the in-game environment so we could see the people we are playing with.

If even 1 and 2 are fixed (or even just point 1!) I will change this review to 5 stars, as I genuinely love this game. It just needs a few tweaks to fix frustrating elements and it will be all Zen!
Cheers!

I play a few rounds of this game daily. Nice way to unwind after work. Graphics are good and work well in VR. You teleport around so no chance of VR sickness if you do suffer from it. Couple things I’d like to see updated. I would like the option to have my tiles hover above my head so I look up to select them and they don’t block my view of the board. The other change I’d like is to select replay and retain the settings from the previous round. I would simply need to position my stone and the others would auto fill in based on my choices made from the start. Lastly I’d like to see at least two more playing environments. Overall a nice relaxing puzzler.

I love this game and would gladly give it five stars if multiplayer was not so frustrating. First of all you can't turn off hand tracking and game so if you're controller sit still for too long it will think you're on hand tracking and then it will think you're pinching your fingers together and keep kicking you out of the match. Somewhat easy fix just go into Oculus settings and disable hand tracking. But then you also leave the match just by holding the controller in too long which my 60 to 70-year-old parents kept doing. I guess you can be in many games at once for some reason that was also causing problems for us. It would be nice if I could just send out invites they could get a pop up in game to accept or decline if they accept they join my game. Add a button or a door to go through to leave the match! I refuse to play with my family anymore because this was the most frustrating time I have ever had in VR and I bought the Vive,rift, quest on release day

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear that. We will investigate an option to disable hand tracking.
For the multiplayer, I recommend launching the app from an Oculus Party - that way you will all start in the multiplayer game with voice chat, and you can keep rematching!

reviews.review_description:
I have played the board game so I knew what to expect from a gameplay perspective. You can teleport to various rooftops to get a birds eye view of the board which is nice. You only need 1 controller and you use your thumb and index finger for play.  Enjoyable game with strategy and its relaxing like everyone says.  Calming environment and music. 
  It does need a few more things in the tutorial. It wasn’t intuitive that you had to point at the banners to access multiplayer or change game rules, but I stumbled across it. Multiplayer is supported and works fine with a few quirks. No microphone support to talk to your game mates. Game management as far as starting a rematch could be simpler. 
  All in all quite enjoyable and I appreciate that this game was brought to VR and done well. Thanks devs!

Update: you can voice chat in the game if you first create an Oculus party and invite your friends and then you each separately start the game. Using oculus parties requires a linked facebook account however.

developer_response.body: Hello! Thanks for the ace review - glad you worked out how to play with Voice Chat. You should be able to launch the game together from an Oculus Party... but this is a new feature from Oculus that we preempted, as it wasn't available at time of launch. Top tip: you can start a rematch very quickly by hitting the rematch button on the Online Scroll after a game. :D

reviews.review_description: One of my favorite board games! Like I wish you could move where the tiles in your "hand" are in space but other than that I have no complaints!

I would give it 5 stars for the graphics and relaxing zen environment, but the multiplayer is really bad. The only way to play and chat with your friends is to start a party and then everyone needs to open Tsuro on their side. It is not intuitive and I wish that the way it is implemented could work as in WBMG (mini golf) where you can hit on multiplayer button and it connects you with someone available to play in direct instead of play and pass mode.
